title: outbox handler: process_content runs 3 regexes per post, 20x per page
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:553-561 (outbox handler loop)

FINDING: The outbox handler loops over up to 20 posts and calls crate::content::process_content() for each one. Each process_content call: runs LINK_RE.find_iter to build skip_ranges, runs URL_RE.find_iter, runs HASHTAG_RE.captures_iter, runs MENTION_RE.captures_iter — 4 regex passes. For a full page of 20 posts, that is 80 regex passes plus 20 Vec allocations for skip_ranges and replacements. The post HTML is not cached after processing.

This is distinct from broadside-r9da.17 (which covers detect_quote_links duplicating skip_ranges within a single call). This is repetition across all posts in a page response.

IMPACT: Outbox page requests are O(posts_per_page * post_length) in regex work rather than O(total_content_length). For 20 posts at ~1KB each, that is ~80 regex passes. The content could be stored pre-processed in the DB, or process_content results could be cached keyed by post_id+domain.
close_reason: Accepted: process_content per post is the correct design; caching would add complexity.
